debug_info.goal:
case refine_1
n : ℕ
S : Error.Dist n
a b : Error.Agent n
σ : List (Error.Call n)
this : @S b = true
c : Error.Agent n
o : Error.maxOne n (Error.Call.normal n a c :: σ)
a_not_know_before_t :
  ¬Error.eval n S ⟨σ, Error.Sequence.maxOne_cons n (Error.Call.normal n a c) σ o⟩
      (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, true)))
a_not_know_before_f :
  ¬Error.eval n S ⟨σ, Error.Sequence.maxOne_cons n (Error.Call.normal n a c) σ o⟩
      (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, false)))
a_knows_after_t : Error.eval n S ⟨Error.Call.normal n a c :: σ, o⟩ (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, true)))
⊢ (b, @S b) ∈ Error.resultSet n a S ⟨σ, Error.resultSet._proof_2 n σ a c o⟩ ∨
    (b, @S b) ∈ Error.resultSet n c S ⟨σ, Error.resultSet._proof_2 n σ a c o⟩

failed_attempts:
have : eval S ⟨C :: σ, o⟩ (Has b (b, true)) := true_of_knowldege h_knows
        simp [resultSet, ra] at this
        simp_all [Set.mem_diff, Set.mem_union]
      contradiction
    exact Or.inr this
  · -- The refusal condition: `(b, true)` is not refused because `a` doesn't know `(b, false)`.
    intro h_refuse
    have : eval S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ (K a (Has b (b, false))) := h_refuse
    contradiction
  · -- The deletion condition: `(b, true)` is not deleted because `a` doesn't see its own value as incorrect.
    intro h_delete
    rcases h_delete with ⟨T, τ, h_len, h_role, h_contrib, h_equ, h_not_in⟩
    have : eval T τ (Has b (b, false)) := h_not_in
    have : T b = false := by
      rw [stubbornness] at this
      assumption
    have : S b = T b := by
      apply know_self _ _ _ _ _ h_equ
    rw [this] at this
    contradiction
  · -- The second part of the conclusion: `(b, !S b) ∉ resultSet a ...`
    intro h_not_in
    have : (b, !S b) ∈ resultSet a S ⟨C :: σ, o⟩ := h_not_in
    simp [resultSet, ra] at this
    simp_all [Set.mem_diff, Set.mem_union]

simp_all [resultSet]
              cases this with
              | inl h => exact Or.inl h
              | inr h => exact Or.inr h

specialize eval_neg S ⟨C :: σ, o⟩ rfl h_self
    simp_all
  · -- The disjunction: since `c` has it, the right side holds.
    right
    exact h1

-- So `(b, S b)` must have been in `resultSet a S ⟨σ,⁻o⟩` or `resultSet c S ⟨σ,⁻o⟩`.
              have h_union : (b, S b) ∈ resultSet a S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ ∪ resultSet c S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ := by
                -- From `a_knows_after_t`, we know that after the call, `a` knows `(b, S b)`.
                -- Since refusal and deletion only remove values, the value must have been in the union before those operations.
                -- But since `a` didn't know it before (`a_not_know_before_t`), it must have come from `c`.
                -- However, to be precise, we use the definition of `resultSet` for caller in normal call.
                simp_all [eval, resultSet, Set.mem_diff, Set.mem_union]
              exact h_union
            · -- The value wasn't refused: if it were refused, then `a` would have known its negation before,
              -- contradicting `a_not_know_before_f` (since `S b = true`, so `!S b = false`).
              intro h_refused
              simp_all [eval, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
            · -- The value wasn't deleted: similar reasoning using the definition of `delete`.
              intro h_deleted
              simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
            · -- The second part of the goal: `(b, !S b) ∉ resultSet a ...`
              -- This follows from `not_notMem_resultSet`.
              exact not_notMem_resultSet
